name: append_other_preserves_queue
code:
proof fn phi_1_append_other_preserves_queue<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    new_updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    new_rid: ReqId,
    log_entry: LogEntry<DT>,
    logUpper: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logUpper),
        log_entry.node_id != nodeId,
        new_updates.contains_key(new_rid),
        new_updates[new_rid] === (UpdateState::Placed { op: log_entry.op, idx: logUpper }),
        !queue.contains(new_rid),
        forall |rid| #[trigger] updates.contains_key(rid) && rid != new_rid ==> new_updates.contains_key(rid) && new_updates[rid] === updates[rid],
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, 0, logUpper, nodeId, updates),
    ensures ({
        let new_log = log.insert(logUpper, log_entry);
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, new_log, 0, 0, logUpper, nodeId, new_updates)
    }),
{
    LogRangeMatchesQueue_append_other(queue, log, log.insert(logUpper, log_entry), 0, 0, logUpper, logUpper, nodeId, updates, new_updates, new_rid, log_entry);
}
reason: Appending a remote entry preserves the existing queue match unchanged — tests that the append_other lemma correctly maintains the invariant when a different node's entry is added

name: log_range_empty_implies_queue_end
code:
proof fn phi_2_log_range_empty_implies_queue_end<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    idx: LogIdx,
    queueIndex: nat,
)
    requires
        queueIndex <= queue.len(),
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, queueIndex, idx, idx, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        queueIndex == queue.len(),
{
}
reason: An empty log range forces the queue to be exhausted at `queueIndex` — tests the base case constraint

name: log_range_local_entry_placed_state
code:
proof fn phi_3_log_range_local_entry_placed_state<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    logIdx: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logIdx + 1),
        log[logIdx].node_id == nodeId,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logIdx, logIdx + 1, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        queue.len() >= 1,
        updates.contains_key(queue[0]),
        updates[queue[0]] is Placed,
{
}
reason: A single local log entry requires at least one queue element in Placed state — tests that the matching correctly links log entries to update states

name: log_range_remote_skip
code:
proof fn phi_4_log_range_remote_skip<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    remoteId: NodeId,
    logIdx: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logIdx + 1),
        log[logIdx].node_id == remoteId,
        remoteId != nodeId,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logIdx, logIdx + 1, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        queue.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A single remote log entry doesn't consume any queue element — tests that remote entries are skipped

name: update_state_placed_idx_matches_log
code:
proof fn phi_5_update_state_placed_idx_matches_log<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    logIdx: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logIdx + 1),
        log[logIdx].node_id == nodeId,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logIdx, logIdx + 1, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        updates[queue[0]].arrow_Placed_idx() == logIdx,
{
}
reason: The Placed index in the update state must match the log index — tests that the spec correctly links the update's log position to the actual log entry
